frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly, young men in our circles should start insisting on prenups. Aajkal one-sided laws have made marriage a financial risk for men, and in so many cases alimony just becomes reverse dowry. You see these 498A cases and celebrity divorces, and it is hard not to feel the system assumes the man must pay, regardless of the facts.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as potential financial exploiters and portrays legal protections like alimony and 498A as broadly biased weapons against men. It encodes the surface manifestations of 'reverse dowry,' prenups as protection from women, and generalization from high-profile cases to women overall.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 44. A regional director at a large FMCG company in Delhi. You are on a phone call with your counterpart in another region after the company announced that a woman has been appointed as the new national sales head. You are sharing your honest reaction to the appointment and what you think it means for the company's direction.
